hardware: c8a.4xlarge (AMD Zen5 EPYC 9R45, 16 vCPU) on-demand, ap-northeast-1
change: fixed256 hamming(): try_from length checks -> debug_assert + unsafe pointer cast to &[u64;4]. The width is the build's contract (fixed256 = 256-bit codes, user-guaranteed); re-proving it per call cost real dispatch slots even after 071 (the check sat inside the 80M-iteration j-loop).
gain: +17% QPS over 071 at identical recall; C=2000 crosses 1000 QPS at 10M on 16 vCPU. CV <= 0.9%.
negative_control: A bundled variant (stack acc array + per-tile query pre-conversion + cfg-split loop) measured 927/887 — SLOWER than 071 despite removing the same checks: the raw-pointer &[u64;4] array and split loop broke LLVM's aliasing/unroll analysis. Only the minimal in-kernel cast wins; the lesson of 012/071 (give the compiler its structure) still binds everywhere outside the cast.

kernel_arc_c2000.note: +68% cumulative from kernel-only changes, recall bit-identical throughout
